text.title: CLI vs SDK — Default to CLI, Switch on Concrete Trigger
text.summary: Pick the Claude Code CLI as the default agent runtime; only drop to the Agent SDK when one of four concrete triggers fires.

section.title: The default rule
section.p: The Claude Code CLI bundles the full agent harness (filesystem, bash, git, MCP, plan mode, sub-agents, hooks, skills, compaction). Re-implementing this in a custom SDK loop is almost always overkill for coding/devops/research work.

rule.statement: For any new agent that runs a coding, devops, or research task on a machine with a shell, the first implementation MUST be a `claude -p` subprocess invocation. A custom Agent SDK loop is allowed only after the CLI has been demonstrated to fail one of the four switch-triggers below.
rule.rationale: The CLI harness encodes battle-tested decisions (retry, compaction, tool gating) maintained by Anthropic. Building a custom SDK loop first re-pays that cost without measurable benefit.

section.reference: Anthropic — Claude Code headless mode docs.

section.title: The four switch-triggers
section.p: Drop to the Agent SDK only when ONE of these is true. Document which trigger fired in the agent's design doc.

list.item: Non-coding domain that does not benefit from filesystem + git + bash (e.g. claims processing, sales triage).
Custom tools that cannot be expressed as an MCP server.
Programmatic control of the inner loop (branch conversation tree, intercept every tool call, custom replay).
Embedding inside a SaaS product whose host process cannot fork subprocesses.

antipattern.description: Picking the Agent SDK first because "it's the API" or "the CLI looks like a CLI tool, not a runtime."
antipattern.reason: Re-implements months of harness work; loses MCP/plan-mode/hooks/compaction; adds maintenance burden every time Anthropic ships a CLI improvement.

example.code: Task: "fix flaky test in tests/test_auth.py" Domain? coding ✓ Triggers? none ✓ → use `claude -p` (CLI default)
example.note: Coding task, no SDK trigger fired — CLI is correct.

example.code: Task: "fix flaky test in tests/test_auth.py" Decision: write a 400-line Python loop using anthropic.Anthropic() calling tools manually.
example.note: Re-implements file/edit/bash/git tools that the CLI already ships. No SDK trigger justifies this.
